name.text: Finding the area of a trapezium

questiontext.text:
A trapezium has parallel sides of lengths {@a@} cm and {@b@} cm. The non-parallel sides are each {@slant@} cm.

[[jsxgraph]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-2, 20, 30, -8], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); // Inject STACK variables var a = {#a#}; var b = {#b#}; var h = {#h#}; var slant = {#slant#}; // Geometry var x_offset = 2; var diff = (a - b) / 2; // Points var p1 = board.create('point', [x_offset, 0], {name: '', visible: false}); var p2 = board.create('point', [x_offset + a, 0], {name: '', visible: false}); var p3 = board.create('point', [x_offset + a - diff, h], {name: '', visible: false}); var p4 = board.create('point', [x_offset + diff, h], {name: '', visible: false}); // Polygon board.create('polygon', [p1, p2, p3, p4], { fillColor: '#f0f0f0', borders: {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2} }); // Labels board.create('text', [x_offset + a/2, -0.5, a + " cm"]); board.create('text', [x_offset + a/2, h + 0.5, b + " cm"]); board.create('text', [x_offset + diff/2 - 1.5, h/2, slant + " cm"]); board.create('text', [x_offset + a - diff/2 + 0.5, h/2, slant + " cm"]); // Height line board.create('segment', [p4, [x_offset + diff, 0]], { dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray' }); board.create('text', [x_offset + diff + 0.2, h/2, "h"]); [[/jsxgraph]]

Calculate the area of the trapezium.

Area = [[input:ans1]] cm \(^2\) [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Step 1: Find the base of the side triangle.
Because the trapezium is isosceles, the difference between the parallel sides is shared equally:

\[ \text{Side base} = \frac{{@a@} - {@b@}}{2} = {@diff_single@} \text{ cm} \]

Step 2: Calculate height (\(h\)) using Pythagoras.

\[ h^2 + {@diff_single@}^2 = {@slant@}^2 \] \[ h^2 + {@diff_single^2@} = {@slant^2@} \] \[ h^2 = {@h_sq@} \implies h = {@h@} \text{ cm} \]

Step 3: Calculate the Area.

\[ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2}(a + b)h \] \[ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2}({@a@} + {@b@}) \times {@h@} \] \[ \text{Area} = {@ta1@} \text{ cm}^2 \]

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
